items.title: System Flowchart
items.caption: This diagram shows the full control logic of the smart farm, from startup and sensor reads to access control, lighting, humidifier behavior, alerts, display updates, and cloud upload.
points: The ESP32 starts by initializing the DHT11, LDR sensors, RFID reader, LCD display, relay, servo, LEDs, and humidifier modules.
The system continuously checks RFID access, temperature and humidity status, ambient darkness, and the laser fence condition.
If the environment needs correction, the humidifier and lighting actions are triggered automatically without manual input.
If an unauthorized card or fence interruption is detected, the system keeps the gate closed and raises an alert.
Each loop ends by updating the display and sending the latest monitoring data to the IoT cloud.

items.title: Circuit Diagram
items.caption: This hardware layout shows how the ESP32 is connected to the LCD, RFID reader, DHT11, LDR modules, relay, servo, power module, water pump, lighting LEDs, and laser fence parts.
points: The ESP32 acts as the controller and coordinates all sensing, security, and automation decisions.
The relay module switches the humidifier path, while the servo motor controls the gate movement after RFID authorization.
The fence lighting LEDs and farm lighting LEDs are separated so visibility and environmental lighting can be managed independently.
The XL4016 step-down module helps regulate the power path from the battery to the lower-voltage components in the system.
The laser module and LDR pair work together as a virtual fence for intrusion detection.

items.title: Future Demo Video
items.caption: This placeholder shows the structure for later Cloudinary video uploads. Publish it when you have a demo clip ready.
points: Videos will render with a native player and Cloudinary delivery URL.
You can optionally set a posterPublicId for a custom thumbnail.
Keep preload light so the showcase stays fast.
